todaysEvents.summary: US sent 15-point peace plan to Iran via Pakistan on Mar 25, demanding nuclear rollback, enrichment halt, and dismantling of Natanz/Isfahan/Fordo; Iran to receive end of all sanctions and civil nuclear assistance at Bushehr in return; Iran has not yet formally responded (NYT report cited by CNBC, France24, Xinhua). (Mar 25)

Iran FM announced non-hostile vessels may transit Hormuz 'in coordination with competent Iranian authorities' on Mar 25 — first meaningful Hormuz de-escalation signal; selective passage framework remains; non-hostile states only (France24, Canberra Times). Brent +4% to ~$104.49 on Mar 24 close (CNBC/Fortune). (Mar 25)
todaysEvents.delta: Iran opens Hormuz to non-hostile ships

todaysEvents.summary: Israel launched new wave of strikes on Tehran infrastructure on Mar 25, with IDF citing 'terror regime targets in the heart of Tehran'; first-ever IDF strikes in northern Iran near Caspian Sea also confirmed; Iran not yet formally responded to US peace plan (IDF statement, CNBC). (Mar 25)
todaysEvents.delta: Israel new Tehran strikes; Caspian first

scenarios.label: Base
scenarios.description: US 15-point peace plan (sent Mar 25 via Pakistan) receives a constructive Iranian response; Hormuz non-hostile passage framework expands into a commercially viable transit corridor within 2 weeks. Brent retreats to $90-100/bbl on confirmed de-escalation; JKM pullback to $18-20 as Qatar Ras Laffan damage is assessed. P&I cover restoration begins within 30 days; Saudi Arabia and Iran agree bilateral standstill. ASEAN oil-importer FX stabilises. Key risk: Israel continued strikes on Tehran suggest military track may not track diplomatic track.

scenarios.label: Stress
scenarios.description: Iran delays or rejects US 15-point plan; Trump's Mar 28 deadline passes without substantive progress. US refrains from power plant strikes but Hormuz non-hostile framework remains narrow — commercial shipping still functionally blocked. Israel continues strikes on Tehran; IRGC maintains low-intensity attacks on US/Saudi targets. Brent settles $100-120/bbl; JKM at $20-24/MMBtu with no Qatar Ras Laffan restart path. ASEAN oil-importer FX (IDR, PHP) under sustained pressure; iTraxx Asia IG at 170-185bp.

scenarios.label: Tail
scenarios.description: Iran formally rejects US 15-point plan; Trump resumes power plant strikes after Mar 28 deadline, triggering IRGC full escalation. Iran revokes non-hostile Hormuz framework; full commercial blockade reinstated. Ras Laffan permanently offline for the quarter; Strait of Malacca closure threat activated. Oil hits $160-190/bbl; LNG spot markets seize. ASEAN recession risk surges — Thailand and Philippines face double-digit fuel inflation and sovereign credit-rating action. Israel's Caspian Sea strikes (Mar 25) signal potential northern front expansion.
